title: Comforting Chinese Pork Dumplings
description: A traditional Zhejiang dish featuring ground ginger and juicy pork, perfect for a sick day, cooked in an iron teppan and served in a papillote, this recipe is low in carbs and full of flavor.

ingredients.name: ground pork
ingredients.notes: preferably with some fat for flavor

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, ground ginger, green onions, soy sauce, and sesame oil, and mix well until just combined.
Lay a wonton wrapper on a flat surface and place a small spoonful of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wrapper, then fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point.
Press the edges together to seal the dumpling, and repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat the iron teppan over medium-high heat and add a small amount of oil to the pan.
When the oil is hot, add the dumplings to the pan, being careful not to overcrowd, and cook until the bottoms are golden brown.
Add the chicken broth to the pan and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pan with a papillote.
Simmer the dumplings for 10-12 minutes, or until they are cooked through and the yeast has activated, giving the dish a yeasty flavor.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with additional green onions and soy sauce if desired.
